research.title: Extending EOL/EOS Software Intelligence Across Containers, Kubernetes, and Modern Workloads
research.summary: Key Takeaways Unsupported software increasingly exists inside container images and Kubernetes workloads, not just traditional infrastructure. Lifecycle risk extends beyond CVEs because unsupported software eventually stops receiving patches and vendor.

vulns.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Drupal Core contains a SQL injection vulnerability that could allow for privilege escalation and remote code execution via specially crafted requests sent with the database abstraction API.
vulns.technicalSummary: CVE-2026-9082 is a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability affecting Drupal core sites configured to use PostgreSQL as the backend database. The vulnerability exists in Drupal’s database abstraction API, where specially crafted requests can bypass.
vulns.breakdown: CVE-2026-9082 is a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability affecting Drupal core sites configured to use PostgreSQL as the backend database. The vulnerability exists in Drupal’s database abstraction API, where specially crafted requests can bypass.
vulns.whatBroke: Drupal Core contains a SQL injection vulnerability that could allow for privilege escalation and remote code execution via specially crafted requests sent with the database abstraction API.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at thinhap/CVE-2026-9082-PoC.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/thinhap/CVE-2026-9082-PoC
cd CVE-2026-9082-PoC
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: initial access through exposed Core
credential access from service context
webshell or command execution follow-up
internal pivot from an internet-facing control point
lateral movement if secrets or management access are recovered
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Exploit Development Lab: 12 progressive classes from stack buffer overflow to Windows kernel exploitation.
vulns.summary: Exploit Development Lab: 12 progressive classes from stack buffer overflow to Windows kernel exploitation (PreviousMode + token steal). Each class: vulnerable C source + working Python exploit.
vulns.technicalSummary: Exploit Development Lab: 12 progressive classes from stack buffer overflow to Windows kernel exploitation (PreviousMode + token steal). Each class: vulnerable C source + working Python exploit.

vulns.title: nginx ngx_http_rewrite_module Heap Buffer Overflow
vulns.summary: NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_rewrite_module module. This vulnerability exists when a rewrite directive uses a regex pattern with distinct, overlapping Perl-Compatible Regular Expression.
vulns.technicalSummary: NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_rewrite_module module. This vulnerability exists when a rewrite directive uses a regex pattern with distinct, overlapping Perl-Compatible Regular Expression.
